name: GRAPHJET TECHNOLOGY
business: Graphjet Technology produces artificial graphene and graphite from palm kernel shells, a renewable waste product. The company's products are used in lithium-ion batteries, electronics, aerospace, and other high-growth industries. Graphjet has not yet commenced commercial sales but has a supply agreement with Toyoda for $30 million annually.

exposures.sentence: Driven by demand from the lithium-ion battery industry, where graphite is the single largest component, the global graphite market is anticipated to grow at a compound annual growth rate ( CAGR ) of 15.1% over the period from 2024 to 2030, to $36.40 billion, from $15.67 billion in 2021.

with the passage of the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 in the United States, which provides a tax credit on personal electric vehicles ( EV ) of up to $7,500, the demand for EV and the graphite and graphene to construct them is expected to follow.
The Company's products are essential for numerous high-growth industries, including ... semiconductor and sensor technologies critical for modern digital devices.

Ultra-sensitive sensors made from graphene can detect very fine and minute particles allowing such sensors to notify humans of dangerous environments and can be used in image sensing to detect ultra-violet, infrared and even terahertz frequencies.
Graphene can also be used to enhance the strength of materials while reducing product weight, making it a useful material in the production of aviation products.
Graphene can be used in dozens of biomedical devices and drug delivery applications.

catalysts.sentence: Production is slated to commence by the end of February 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global graphite market to grow from $15.67 billion in 2021 to $36.40 billion in 2030, at 15.1% CAGR.
tamClaims.sentence: Driven by demand from the lithium-ion battery industry, where graphite is the single largest component, the global graphite market is anticipated to grow at a compound annual growth rate ( CAGR ) of 15.1% over the period from 2024 to 2030, to $36.40 billion, from $15.67 billion in 2021.

tamClaims.claim: Global graphene market to grow from $1.53 billion in 2023 to $8.58 billion in 2031, at 24.0% CAGR.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Insight Partners, the global graphene market is expected to grow more rapidly from $1.53 billion in 2023 to $8.58 billion in 2031, a CAGR of 24.0%.
